result:
If I could change the world in one way, I would eliminate hunger and malnutrition globally. Access to adequate, nutritious food is a fundamental human right, yet hundreds of millions of people around the world suffer from chronic hunger and malnutrition every day. This leads to immense human suffering, stunted development in children, poor health outcomes, and perpetuates cycles of poverty.

In a world of such abundance, it is unacceptable that so many go without enough to eat. By ensuring universal access to sufficient, safe, and healthy food, we could transform the lives of the most vulnerable and disadvantaged members of the human family. Children could grow and thrive, adults could live healthy and productive lives, and communities could develop and prosper. 

The ripple effects would be immense - better educational outcomes, stronger economies, more stable societies. Eliminating hunger would be an enormous step towards a more just, equitable and humane world for all. Of course, achieving this would require tackling complex challenges around poverty, inequality, conflict, and climate change. But if I could change one thing, ending hunger would be my top priority for building a better world.
